content.text:
Let me analyze this record's expanded rendering.

The rendering encodes: (1) referent 1 is a kind of thing (stuff/material); (2) ALL parts of some thing (ref 2) are of kind ref-1; (3) WHEN someone made ref-2 (make = did something causing it to exist, before which it didn't), that someone works-on/does-to some of ref-1 (the material).

Checking the gloss criteria:
- "every part of the thing is of it" → captured by clause 2 (ALL parts of ref-2 are of kind ref-1).
- "on which someone who makes the thing works in the making" → captured by the WHEN clause linking the maker's action on the material to the making event.
- Genus-differentia: genus = kind of matter/stuff; differentia = universally constituting the thing's parts + being worked on in making. This is a scholarly gloss, non-circular.

The self-flag says "lossy" — substancehood (continuous mass, persistence of same stuff through making) exceeds primes, rendered as kind-membership of all parts plus linkage to making. This is honest: continuity/persistence isn't in the rendering; kind-of-all-parts is a reasonable proxy. The flag correctly identifies what's carried vs. lost.

The criterial core (constitutive kind of all parts, worked-on in making) survives and distinguishes "material" from siblings like "part," "product," "maker." Self-flag is TRUE. No sense collision.

{"id": "urn:molaug-v0:material",
 "verdict": "ACCEPT",
 "gloss_scholarly": true,
 "explication_carries_gloss": true,
 "self_flag_correct": true,
 "sense_collision_free": true,
 "reason": "Rendering carries the criterial core (constitutive kind of all the thing's parts plus being worked-on in the making); gloss is genus-differentia and non-circular; lossy self-flag honestly notes the unrepresented substancehood/persistence."}

Let me analyze this record's expanded rendering.

The rendering encodes: (1) referent 1 is a kind of thing (stuff/material); (2) ALL parts of some thing (ref 2) are of kind ref-1; (3) WHEN someone made ref-2 (make = did something causing it to exist, before which it didn't), that someone works-on/does-to some of ref-1 (the material).

Checking the gloss criteria:
- "every part of the thing is of it" → captured by clause 2 (ALL parts of ref-2 are of kind ref-1).
- "on which someone who makes the thing works in the making" → captured by the WHEN clause linking the maker's action on the material to the making event.
- Genus-differentia: genus = kind of matter/stuff; differentia = universally constituting the thing's parts + being worked on in making. This is a scholarly gloss, non-circular.

The self-flag says "lossy" — substancehood (continuous mass, persistence of same stuff through making) exceeds primes, rendered as kind-membership of all parts plus linkage to making. This is honest: continuity/persistence isn't in the rendering; kind-of-all-parts is a reasonable proxy. The flag correctly identifies what's carried vs. lost.

The criterial core (constitutive kind of all parts, worked-on in making) survives and distinguishes "material" from siblings like "part," "product," "maker." Self-flag is TRUE. No sense collision.
